avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The sometimes strong wind has transported some snow. The large wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant on steep shady slopes above approximately 2600 m. This applies in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example. The avalanches can in isolated cases reach large size.
On slopes small and medium-sized wet avalanches are possible as the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 100 to 150 cm of snow, and up to 180 cm in some localities, has fallen since Saturday. The moderate wind has transported the new snow significantly.
The new snow has settled to a large extent at low and intermediate altitudes.
Intermediate and high altitudes: The new snow of the weekend is poorly bonded with the old snowpack in many places.
The surface of the snowpack will soften during the day. In the event of solar radiation this applies in particular at low and intermediate altitudes as well as.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in isolated cases in particular on very steep shady slopes at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions. This applies in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example. The wind slabs are clearly recognisable to the trained eye.
On slopes small and medium-sized wet avalanches are possible as the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day.

snowpackStructure.comment:
10 to 15 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell yesterday above approximately 1700 m. The moderate wind has transported the new snow significantly.
The new snow has settled to a large extent at low and intermediate altitudes.
Intermediate and high altitudes: The surface of the snowpack will soften during the day. This applies in particular on sunny slopes, as well as at low and intermediate altitudes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Slab avalanches are the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In particular on steep slopes medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large slab avalanches are possible as a consequence of new snow and strong wind.
The fresh and somewhat older wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in isolated cases on steep shady slopes above approximately 2000 m. This applies in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example. The wind slabs are clearly recognisable to the trained eye.
On slopes small and medium-sized wet avalanches are possible as the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day.

snowpackStructure.comment:
10 to 15 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell yesterday above approximately 1700 m.
The easterly wind has transported the fresh and old snow. The various wind slabs have bonded with the old snowpack.
